papers.title: TraderBench: How Robust Are AI Agents in Adversarial Capital Markets?
papers.innovation: Introduces TraderBench, a novel benchmark for evaluating the robustness of AI agents in dynamic and adversarial capital markets, addressing limitations of static benchmarks and uncontrolled variance from LLM-based judges.
papers.use_case: Developing, evaluating, and stress-testing robust AI trading agents; assessing the resilience of AI systems in high-stakes, dynamic financial environments; guiding research into secure and reliable financial AI.

papers.title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
papers.innovation: Proposes EmCoop, a comprehensive framework and benchmark to facilitate the development and evaluation of multiple embodied LLM agents capable of complex cooperation in dynamic environments, addressing the challenges of high-level cognitive coordination under embodied constraints.
papers.use_case: Designing and testing collaborative multi-agent robotic systems; training and evaluating embodied AI for complex real-world tasks; simulating and developing advanced LLM-based coordination strategies for autonomous agents.

papers.title: MED-COPILOT: A Medical Assistant Powered by GraphRAG and Similar Patient Case Retrieval
papers.innovation: Introduces MED-COPILOT, a medical assistant that enhances LLM reasoning by combining GraphRAG with a novel similar patient case retrieval mechanism. This approach addresses LLM hallucinations and difficulties in integrating long, structured clinical evidence for robust clinical decision-making.
papers.benchmarks: Implicitly, clinical reasoning accuracy, reduction of hallucinations, and effectiveness in synthesizing heterogeneous medical evidence.
papers.use_case: Developing reliable and accurate AI assistants for clinical decision support; building production-grade RAG systems for domain-specific knowledge in high-stakes applications like healthcare; improving LLM performance on complex, evidence-based reasoning tasks.
